Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration, Enforcing Contractual Choice Of Venue And Choice Of Law Provisions And Consolidating Related Cases
Off Calendar Jul-17-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-13-296471 The Michelle Woodhouse Trust, PTR-13-296472 The Barry Woodhouse Trust, PTR-13-296473 The Stephen Woodhouse Trust, and PES-13-296527 In Re: Brian J. Keenan.

Appearances: Stephanie Dobozin and Donald Putterman for Delia Foster, Barry Woodhouse and Michelle Woodhouse. Sheila Robello for Train Babcock in the Woodhouse matters and for John Rogicki and Train Babcock Advisors in the Keenan matter. Attorney Gary Botto was also present.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not the arbitration clause of the Investment Advisory Agreement applies. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court indicated that its tentative ruling is that the arbitration clause does not apply. The Court granted Ms. Robello's request for a Statement of Decision and asked Counsel to each submit a proposed statement of decision by July 11, 2013. Responses to the underlying petitions are due by August 13, 2013. The Court granted consolidation of the matters but the Clerk pointed out that consolidation is not ordinarly done in Probate. Therefore, the Judicial Officer will discuss this issue with the Probate Examiner. Ms. Dobozin's request to have a citation issued to Brian Keenan was granted. All matters were continued to September 17, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery.

Judge: John Dearman. Court Reporter: Karen Scott, CSR#4027, (925)-389-7314. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Surcharge Issue And Appointment Of Successor Trustee/Agent
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Continued To Sep-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Accounting By Brian Keenan
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Continued To Sep-17-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION Petition For Orders Confirming Termination Of Powers Of Attorney, Compelling Account Of Attorney-Of-Fact, Etc.
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with the following cases; PTR-13-296471 The Michelle Woodhouse Trust, PTR-13-296472 The Barry Woodhouse Trust, PTR-13-296473 The Stephen Woodhouse Trust, and PES-13-296527 In Re: Brian J. Keenan.

Appearances: Stephanie Dobozin and Donald Putterman for Delia Foster and Barry Woodhouse. Sheila Robello and Kevin Solan for Train Babcock and John Rogicki.

Ms. Robello informed the Court that a Petition to Compel Arbitration is set for July 2, 2013. The Court heard oral argument and ruled as follows: Brian Keenan is removed as Trustee/Agent from all of the above listed matters. Brian Keenan is required to file an accounting in all matters. Status on filing of said accountings was set for July 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Tentatively the Court ruled that John Rogicki is suspended from acting as Successor Trustee. Ms. Robello is to contact Mr. Rogicki to see if he is willing to sign a declination. Ms. Robello is to advise opposing counsel as to Mr. Rogicki's decision by May 3, 2013. If Mr. Rogicki refuses to sign a declination, the Court's tentative ruling will become final on May 3, 2013. The Court granted the Petitions to Remove Trustee/Agent and to Compel an Account on all four matters (subject to receipt and review of the orders). Ms. Dobozin is to prepare said orders. The surcharge issue and appointment of Successor Trustee/Agent on all matters is continued to July 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Court Reporter: Karen Scott (CSR#: 4027, Telephone: 415-433-1234). Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy Whitehead.
